title: Bayer Banks on US Supreme Court’s Help to Rein In Roundup Lawsuits
summary: Justices hear appeal of verdict linking weedkiller to cancer as company seeks to curtail litigation that’s been a drag on shares.

title: Raízen Adds Cash in Offer to Creditors, Balks at Board Changes
summary: Raízen SA sent an alternative proposal to creditors as it tries to iron out terms of a 65 billion-real ($13 billion) debt restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: City Shake-Up Bill to Be Included in King’s Speech, FT Reports
summary: The UK government will introduce a major financial services reform bill in the King’s Speech on May 13, the Financial Times reported, citing officials briefed on the measure.

title: Suspect in DC Dinner Attack Spent Several Years Acquiring Guns
summary: The man accused of storming the White House Correspondents’ Dinner spent years quietly acquiring his arsenal, purchasing a shotgun from a Torrance, California, firearms dealer eight months before the attack and a semi-automatic pistol two years earlier, according to a law enforcement intelligence profile reviewed by Bloomberg.

title: Adidas Gets Running Lift in London From Two Sub-2 Hour Marathons
summary: As athletes and spectators marvel at, not one, but two men smashing the once-thinkable two-hour barrier in this year’s London Marathon, German sportsgear maker Adidas AG also has reason to celebrate.

title: What’s Happening in EM: Frontiers to Build on Rally (Podcast)
summary: Malavika Kaur Makol and Bhaskar Dutta discuss how frontier market assets are set to build on their rally in April, as demand picks up after the initial selloff due to the Iran war.

summary: Germany sounded out European lenders to defend Commerzbank AG against UniCredit SpA’s takeover bid earlier this year, according to people familiar with the matter.

summary: Germany’s Hensoldt AG, a supplier of radar and sensor technology, doesn’t expect Chinese export curbs to have any material impact on its operations or outlook.

title: King Charles' Security Under Review ahead of DC State Visit
summary: Buckingham Palace says it is discussing whether Saturday's shooting at the Washington Hilton will impact "operational planning" for King Charles' first State visit to the United States. The King and Queen are scheduled to meet with President Trump for a State Dinner at this White House this week before traveling to New York City. Bloomberg's Mishal Husain discusses the new security challenge and global reaction to the shooting in Washington on "Bloomberg This Weekend." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: History of Presidential Assassination Attempts
summary:
In the aftermath of the shooting at the White House Correspondents's dinner, Bloomberg's Joe Mathieu joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the history of assassination attempts on US presidents how such events have shaped security protocols in Washington, DC.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How Boring Businesses Create Billionaires
summary: Forget tech startups and stock picks. A Princeton economist's research reveals that many of America's millionaires built their fortunes through private businesses in industries most people overlook. Billionaire Rich Kinder did it with pipelines. Ray and Dana Chery are hoping to become the next generation of billionaires by betting on portable sinks. Their stories challenge conventional wisdom on how wealth is actually built. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Investors Protecting Stocks at Record Eye Bets on Higher Rates
summary: As stocks hit record after record, some investors are taking to heart the saying: Hedge when you can, not when you must.

title: Secret Service Likely to Lead Investigation into WHCD Shooting Suspect
summary: Due to the involvement of presidential protection, the Secret Service, with the support of the FBI, is expected to lead the investigation into the suspected shooter. Former Federal, Local and State Police Office Roy Taylor and CNN Legal Analyst Elliot Williams discuss with David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend possible next steps. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Big Tech’s $16 Trillion Earnings Week Is Make-Or-Break for Rally
summary: Wall Street’s biggest technology stocks have carried the S&P 500 to record highs even as the war in Iran continues. Now, earnings from a handful of them this week will give investors a read on whether this rally’s sustainable.

title: Shots Fired at White House Correspondents' Dinner
summary: The suspect in the shooting during the White House Correspondents' Dinner has been identified as Cole Allen and is in custody. Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Jeff Mason and Journalist and Author Elliot Williams join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss what is known and what still needs to be learned.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Evacuated After Shooting at Press Dinner, Gunman Detained
summary: US authorities are charging a gunman in a shooting at the hotel that hosted the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, an incident that forced President Donald Trump to be rushed from the stage and the event to be postponed. Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Jeff Mason and State Department Reporter Eric Martin join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Israel President Holds Off Deciding Whether to Pardon Netanyahu
summary: Israeli President Isaac Herzog has decided to shelve, for now, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s request for a pardon in his graft trial, preferring to press the parties to reach an agreement that would terminate the case consensually, his office announced.

title: Can the World’s Economic Firefighter Adapt to the 21st Century?
summary: As wars, trade spats, and AI reshape the global economy, the IMF has held its role as the world’s lender-of-last-resort steady. Much of its governance, including countries’ shares of voting rights, have remained the same over decades, often requiring lending countries to return to the same austerity playbook. Gita Gopinath, former first managing director of the IMF says that the stability is by design in times of greater geopolitical uncertainty while Boston University’s Kevin Gallagher says that some parts of the IMF are due for an upgrade. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Musk Vies to Turn X Into Super App With Banking Tool Near Launch
summary: More than three years after acquiring Twitter, Elon Musk says he’s nearing his long-stated goal of turning it into an “everything app” with a new financial services tool that he pledged to launch for the public this month.

title: Investors Dive Back Into Frontier Markets After April Rally
summary: Investors are piling back into some of the riskiest markets, as demand for frontier assets picks up after an initial war-driven selloff.

title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Halted With Blockades Firmly in Place
summary: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at a near-complete halt, with neither Iran nor the US showing any sign of easing their blockades of maritime traffic.

title: Russia’s Defense Minister Visits North Korea to Meet Top Leaders
summary: Russia’s Defense Minister Andrey Belousov arrived in North Korea to meet political and military leaders as the two nations deepen strategic ties.

title: Fed Set to Lead Uneasy G-7 With Rates Kept on Hold This Week
summary: Policymakers in the US and across the Group of Seven will probably keep interest rates steady this week while watching nervously for signs of higher energy costs fanning inflation.

title: Gunman Detained After Press Dinner Shooting
summary:
President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance were evacuated from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington on Saturday after shots were fired at the hotel hosting the event. A suspect is now in custody and being questioned, Trump said.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: HK Finance Chief Says City’s 2026 IPOs Have Raised $17.9 Billion
summary: Hong Kong initial public offerings have raised more than HK$140 billion ($17.9 billion) this year, with the city maintaining its position as the world’s top IPO venue, Financial Secretary Paul Chan wrote in his weekly blog.

title: Malaysian Protesters Keep Up Pressure on Anti-Graft Chief
summary: Malaysians gathered at a rally in Kuala Lumpur on Saturday, reiterating calls for the establishment of a Royal Commission of Inquiry into allegations involving outgoing anti-graft chief Azam Baki.

title: Gunman Detained After Shooting at Press Dinner
summary: President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance were evacuated from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington Saturday after shots were fired at the hotel where the event was taking place. A suspect is now in custody and being questioned, Trump said. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump’s Hormuz Blockade Has Deepened a Historic Shipping Crisis
summary: In early April — after a month of disruption around one of the world’s most important energy chokepoints — US President Donald Trump wrote that with “a little ​more time, we can easily OPEN THE ​HORMUZ STRAIT, TAKE THE OIL, & MAKE A ⁠FORTUNE.”

title: Trump Scraps US Trip for Iran Talks, Leaving Ceasefire in Limbo
summary: President Donald Trump canceled a planned trip to Pakistan by his top envoys for negotiations over the Iran conflict, raising questions about the durability of the current ceasefire. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hungary’s Next Premier Warns Investors to Shun Orban-Tied Assets
summary: Incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar said he had information that wealthy figures linked to Viktor Orban’s outgoing government were moving assets abroad and called on authorities to detain fleeing “oligarch” families.

title: Cheap Private Credit Funds Draw Bargain Hunters
summary: Bargain hunters are snatching up private lending funds in the stock market, eager to buy assets that look cheap by a key measure.

title: Bloomberg This Weekend 04/25/2026
summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by “Muskism: A Guide for the Perplexed” Co-Authors Ben Tarnoff and Quinn Slobodian, Former US Fifth Fleet Commander for the US Navy Vice Admiral Kevin Donegan , S&P Global Vice Chairman Daniel Yergin, Representative Michael McCaul, Jetr Global Sports + Entertainment Vice President David Francis and Jetr Global Sports + Entertainment Founder and CEO Travis Murphy. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EU Leaders Convene in Cyprus Amid Iran War
summary: European countries need to step up strategically in the Middle East, strike deals with partners across the region and try to bring an end to the Iran war, Cyprus’ foreign minister said Friday. Bloomberg News Chief Europe Correspondent Oliver Crook joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Billion-Barrel Hormuz Oil Shock Is About to Crash Demand
summary: The Strait of Hormuz oil shock has yet to crash demand as the rich world borrows from its stocks and pays up to secure supply. Traders are now sounding the alarm that a harsh adjustment is coming. Bloomberg News Washington Bureau Chief Peggy Collins and White House Correspondent Jeff Mason join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend for a wide-ranging conversation on the latest in politics. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BTW | FIFA World Cup, Chili, Pokémon
summary: Bloomberg This Weekend = BTW = By The Way: Lisa Mateo, Christina Ruffini and David Gura dive into the headlines you may have missed on Bloomberg This Weekend. Watch more here: Watch the show LIVE every Saturday and Sunday morning. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Triumphal Arch: How Trump is Leaving His Mark on DC
summary: Twice the size of the Lincoln Memorial, taller than almost all structures in Washington DC, and the winged figure atop rising some 60 feet high, the equivalent of a six-story building: President Donald Trump’s proposed “Triumphal Arch.” Bloomberg CityLab writer Kriston Capps joins Christina Ruffini in Washington DC to discuss the impact Trump is having on the architecture of the nation's capital. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rep. McCaul: US-Iran Talks Unlikely to Reach Breakthrough
summary: Rep. Michael McCaul (R-TX) joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the US-Iranian talks that were meant to end the conflict that has closed the Strait of Hormuz.  (Source: Bloomberg)
